answer:
The Pylon MCP server enables AI tools like Claude, Cursor, and ChatGPT to interact with Pylon data using the Model Context Protocol [1]. There is an official hosted version provided by Pylon at `https://mcp.usepylon.com` [1], alongside various open-source implementations [2][3][4][5][6][7][8].

Official tools cover core functionality including issue management, accounts, and contacts [1]. Community-developed versions often provide expanded coverage of the full Pylon API, such as knowledge base articles and custom objects [2][4]. Authentication varies by implementation, ranging from standard OAuth 2.0 [1] and API tokens [6] to x402-based micropayments [7].
